title: Upcoming G7 in Evian puts serious strain on France-Switzerland relations
subhead: The summit, scheduled to take place on the French shore of Lake Geneva from June 15 to 17, has required the involvement of Swiss authorities due to delegations transiting through Geneva Airport and a planned demonstration in the city.

title: Nearly 900 arrested in France after PSG's Champions League win
'We'll be back next year for the third!': PSG celebrates second Champions League title with victory party and eye on more trophies
Sweden ramps up aid to households and businesses to tackle energy crisis
subhead: Less than four months before the legislative elections, the liberal conservative right and the far right have announced 17.3 billion kronor (approximately €1.6 billion) worth of support measures, including halving the price of tickets for public transportation.

title: The Chinese women striving to escape societal pressures to marry and have children
subhead: From climbing gyms to specialty bookstores and entrepreneur clubs, several women-focused spaces have opened in China's major cities. These places of solidarity offer women ways to push back against pressures to have children in a country where feminist activism is closely monitored.

title: Cannes rolls out the red carpet for season 4 of 'White Lotus'
subhead: The acclaimed HBO series, a biting satire of the wealthy, used the backdrop of the Cannes Film Festival to shoot its fourth season, set on the Côte d'Azur. The city council is counting on the global success of the show to bring significant economic benefits.

title: Mutual mistrust persists between football and the French far right
subhead: The Rassemblement National has made headlines more for its criticism and racist outbursts toward the national team than for its support during major tournaments. It has also failed to secure high-profile allies within the sport.

The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
subhead: Xenia Fedorova, the former director of the now-banned Russian channel RT France, has free rein to advance the Kremlin's version of events without contradiction across the media empire of far-right billionaire Vincent Bolloré.

title: Sheikha Al-Mayassa Al Thani, the princess spearheading Qatar's artistic ambitions
subhead: A central figure in the country's cultural policy, the emir's sister has skillfully used the acquisition of prestigious works of art, the hosting of fairs and the creation of museums to build valuable diplomatic ties.

title: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
subhead: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.
byline: Isabelle Chaperon and  Marie de Vergès
